title: Why Art Thou Silent And Invisible
content:
Why art thou silent and invisible
    Father of jealousy
    Why dost thou hide thyself in clouds
    From every searching Eye

    Why darkness & obscurity
    In all thy words & laws
    That none dare eat the fruit but from
    The wily serpents jaws
    Or is it because Secresy
    gains females loud applause
meaning:
This evocative piece by William Blake, titled "Why Art Thou Silent And Invisible", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
